ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employment_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employment record */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the individual (e.g., In post, Vacant) */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the record */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group of the individual (e.g., 16-19, 20-24) */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the individual is employed (e.g., London) */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the individual (e.g., Female, Male) */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the individual (e.g., Asian, Black, Mixed) */,
  "headcount" INTEGER /* Number of individuals in the record */,
  "fte" REAL /* Full-time equivalent (FTE) for the individuals */,
  "mean_salary" REAL /* Mean salary for the individuals in the record */,
  "median_salary" REAL /* Median salary for the individuals in the record */,
  "industry_sector" TEXT /* Industry sector of the employment record */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the employment record */,
  "contract_type" TEXT /* Type of contract for the employment record */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the employment data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_records_region FOREIGN KEY ("region") REFERENCES regions ("region_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_records_sex FOREIGN KEY ("sex") REFERENCES genders ("gender_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_records_contract_type FOREIGN KEY ("contract_type") REFERENCES contract_types ("contract_type_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_records_data_source FOREIGN KEY ("data_source") REFERENCES data_sources ("data_source_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_records_job_title FOREIGN KEY ("job_title") REFERENCES job_titles ("job_title_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_records_age_group FOREIGN KEY ("age_group") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_records_industry_sector FOREIGN KEY ("industry_sector") REFERENCES industry_sectors ("sector_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_records_ethnicity FOREIGN KEY ("ethnicity") REFERENCES ethnicities ("ethnicity_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region (e.g., London) */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the region is located */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the region is located */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the region */,
  "region_code" TEXT /* Unique code for each region */,
  "borough" TEXT /* Borough within the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_group_name" TEXT /* Name of the age group (e.g., 16-19, 20-24) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the age group */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Age range for each age group */,
  "age_group_category" TEXT /* Broader age category for each age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genders" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender */,
  "gender_name" TEXT /* Name of the gender (e.g., Female, Male) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the gender */,
  "gender_category" TEXT /* Broader gender category for each gender */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ethnicities" (
  "ethnicity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ethnicity */,
  "ethnicity_name" TEXT /* Name of the ethnicity (e.g., Asian, Black, Mixed) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the ethnicity */,
  "ethnicity_category" TEXT /* Broader ethnicity category for each ethnicity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ethnicity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salaries" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary record */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employment record the salary belongs to */,
  "mean_salary" REAL /* Mean salary for the individuals in the record */,
  "median_salary" REAL /* Median salary for the individuals in the record */,
  "salary_source" TEXT /* Source of the salary data */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for each salary record */,
  "salary_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of salary payments */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salaries_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES employment_records ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "organisation" TEXT /* Organisation the user belongs to */,
  "job_function" TEXT /* Job function of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employment record being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the record */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the record was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method used to access the employment data */,
  "access_purpose" TEXT /* Purpose of accessing the employment data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES employment_records ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data version */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employment record the version belongs to */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the data */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was created */,
  "version_notes" TEXT /* Notes about the changes in the version */,
  "version_status" TEXT /* Status of the data version */,
  "version_comments" TEXT /* Comments about the changes in the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES employment_records ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "industry_sectors" (
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry sector */,
  "sector_name" TEXT /* Name of the industry sector */,
  "sector_description" TEXT /* Description of the industry sector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_titles" (
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "job_title_name" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  "job_title_description" TEXT /* Description of the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contract_types" (
  "contract_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contract type */,
  "contract_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the contract type */,
  "contract_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the contract type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contract_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "data_source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "data_source_description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_source_id")
)
